name: pagination
context: Paginating GitHub API responses ŌĆö Link headers for REST, cursor-based for GraphQL
info: GitHub API list endpoints return paginated results to manage large datasets. The REST API uses RFC 5988 Link headers with page-based or cursor-based parameters. The GraphQL API uses the Relay connection pattern with `first`/`after` and `last`/`before` cursors. Default page size is 30 for REST (max 100). Efficient pagination is essential for staying within [[github/rate-limits]] and retrieving complete datasets. This guide covers both REST and GraphQL pagination patterns with code examples using Octokit, gh CLI, and raw HTTP. See [[github/rest]] and [[github/graphql]] for API conventions.

sections.title: REST Link header pagination
sections.body: REST list endpoints include a `Link` response header with `rel="next"`, `rel="prev"`, `rel="first"`, and `rel="last"` URLs. Follow `rel="next"` until it's absent to fetch all pages. Use `per_page` (max 100) to control page size. The `page` parameter selects a specific page. See [[github/rest]] for API conventions.

sections.title: GraphQL cursor-based pagination
sections.body: GraphQL uses the Relay connection pattern. Pass `first: N, after: $cursor` for forward pagination. Read `pageInfo.hasNextPage` and `pageInfo.endCursor` to determine if more pages exist and where the next page starts. Maximum `first`/`last` value is 100. Each page costs points against [[github/rate-limits]].

sections.title: Octokit auto-pagination
sections.body: Use `octokit.paginate()` to automatically fetch all pages of a REST endpoint. Accepts the same parameters as the underlying method plus `per_page`. Use `octokit.paginate.iterator()` for streaming iteration over pages. Pass a map function as the third argument to transform each page's data during collection.

sections.title: gh CLI --paginate flag
sections.body: The `gh api --paginate` flag automatically follows Link headers and concatenates results. Works for both REST and GraphQL. For GraphQL, the CLI detects `pageInfo` and `endCursor` and re-executes the query. Combine with `--jq` or `-q` for client-side filtering of paginated results.

links.context: REST API uses Link header pagination

related.note: REST pagination uses Link headers and page/per_page parameters

Efficient pagination helps conserve rate limit budget

author: dojo-community
